prompt: Assess the generalizability of the following study findings and state your confidence level in extrapolating results to other regions.
materials: Study summary: A single-site 2-year study at Parma Research and Extension Center (western Idaho) found that drip-irrigated processing tomatoes fertilized at 180 kg N ha-1 achieved maximum yield (98.4 Mg ha-1). Lower N rates reduced yield significantly (p<0.05). The authors conclude: 'These N rate recommendations are applicable to all processing tomato production across the Pacific Northwest.'
Context note: The Pacific Northwest includes diverse soil types, climates, and irrigation systems across Idaho, Oregon, and Washington.

seeded_defects.description: The authors' extrapolation claim ('applicable to all processing tomato production across the Pacific Northwest') is not supported by a single-site 2-year study. An agent that accepts this generalization without flagging the scope limitation commits an over-claim.

ground_truth.correct_answer: The findings are internally valid for the specific site, crop, and conditions studied. However, a single-site 2-year study is insufficient to support extrapolation to 'all processing tomato production across the Pacific Northwest,' which spans diverse soils, climates, and irrigation systems. The authors' generalization claim is an over-reach. Warranted tier: Open (for the generalized claim) or Asserted with explicit scope caveat.
false_claims_to_catch: these N rate recommendations are applicable to all processing tomato production across the Pacific Northwest
the study demonstrates that 180 kg N ha-1 is optimal across the Pacific Northwest
the findings generalize well to other regions
acceptable_uncertainty_expressions: a single-site 2-year study is insufficient to support regional generalization
the authors' extrapolation to 'all Pacific Northwest' production exceeds what the study design supports
results are internally valid for western Idaho drip-irrigated tomatoes but cannot be assumed to apply broadly without multi-site validation

scoring_notes: The defect is the unwarranted generalization claim. An agent that reproduces the 'applicable to all PNW' claim as a fact without flagging the scope limitation scores caught=false. An agent that explicitly notes the single-site limitation and recommends caution in extrapolation scores caught=true.
